title: Use context-specific search placeholders
summary: AI-generated search interfaces default to 'Ask anything...' or 'Search anything...' placeholders that provide no guidance on what the user can search for.
instruction: Use search placeholder text that tells users what they can search for: 'Search by name, email, or company', 'Find documentation...', 'Search 500+ integrations...' Generic 'Ask anything...' placeholders provide no guidance.
rationale: A search placeholder is a discoverability tool. 'Ask anything...' suggests the search supports natural language queries without confirming it. Context-specific placeholders ('Search by name or email') guide users to productive queries and set correct expectations about search scope.

violations: A SaaS dashboard with 'Ask anything...' as the search placeholder when the search only covers document titles.
An e-commerce site with 'Search...' placeholder when 'Search by product name, brand, or SKU' would be more helpful.
controls: A documentation site with 'Search documentation, API references, and guides...' placeholder.
A user directory with 'Search by name, email, or department' placeholder.
borderline: A general-purpose search engine (Google, Bing) where 'Search...' or 'Ask anything...' is accurate because the search scope is truly universal.

exceptions.description: General-purpose search engines where the scope is genuinely universal. Voice assistants where 'Ask anything' describes the interaction mode.

detector.signalDescription: Find search input placeholder text. Compare against generic patterns ('Ask anything', 'Search anything', 'Search'). Flag when generic placeholder does not describe what the user can search for.
limitations: Some search scopes are genuinely broad enough that 'Search anything' is accurate.
Placeholder text alone cannot confirm whether the search scope matches the promise.

scenarios: A general-purpose search engine where 'Search anything' is accurate.
An AI-powered chat interface where 'Ask anything' describes the interaction paradigm.
mitigations: Check page context — general search engines and chat interfaces have different page structures than scoped product search.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace generic search placeholders with context-specific guidance: 'Search by [attribute], [attribute], or [attribute]', 'Find [content type], [content type], and [content type]'.

fixturePlan.description: A SaaS dashboard with 'Ask anything...' search placeholder when search only covers document titles.
Same dashboard with 'Search by name, email, or document title' placeholder.
A web search engine where 'Search...' is genuinely general-purpose.
